reasoning: The market requires inflation in 2025 to fall below 2.5%. Donald Trump won the 2024 election, so the market will resolve normally (not N/A). As of late October 2025, the September 2025 CPI report showed headline YoY inflation at 3.01% (up slightly from previous months, with MoM prints around +0.3%). For YoY inflation to drop below 2.5% by the end of 2025 (or by the Dec 10, 2025 resolution date, which would cover the Oct or Nov data releases), we would need unprecedented consecutive negative MoM prints in headline CPI, which runs counter to current trends. The Manifold market was at 13% in mid-October 2025, before the delayed September CPI release confirmed sticky 3% inflation. The likelihood of a drop below 2.5% is extremely remote, so I estimate 6%.
